text: You have a right to perform your prescribed duty, but you are not entitled to the fruits of action. Never consider yourself the cause of the results of your activities, and never be attached to not doing your duty.
Elevate yourself through the power of your mind, and not degrade yourself, for the mind can be the friend and also the enemy of the self.

O son of Kunti, the nonpermanent appearance of happiness and distress, and their disappearance in due course, are like the appearance and disappearance of winter and summer seasons. They arise from sense perception, O scion of Bharata, and one must learn to tolerate them without being disturbed.

Mind precedes all mental states. Mind is their chief; they are all mind-wrought. If with an impure mind a person speaks or acts, suffering follows him like the wheel that follows the foot of the ox. If with a pure mind a person speaks or acts, happiness follows him like his never-departing shadow.
Let go of the past, let go of the future, let go of the present, and cross over to the farther shore of existence. With mind wholly liberated, you shall come no more to birth and death.
All this, whatever moves in this moving world, is enveloped by God. Therefore, find your enjoyment in renunciation; do not covet what belongs to others.
Learn flawlessly whatever you learn, and having learned it, live flawlessly according to what you have learned.
Objective truth is the only truth; all other truths are subjective and flawed. Therefore, do not utter a word that your conscience knows to be false.
Souls render service to one another (Parasparopagraho Jivanam).

From the torment by the three kinds of suffering (arises) the inquiry into the means of terminating them; if it be said that it is useless because there exist evident means, we reply: No, because these means do not yield absolute and final relief.
philosophy: Samkhya Philosophy (Dualism of Purusha and Prakriti)

text: Supreme felicity is attained by the knowledge about the true nature of the sixteen categories: means of right knowledge, object of right knowledge, doubt, purpose, familiar instance, established tenet, members of a syllogism, confutation, ascertainment, discussion, wrangling, cavil, fallacy, quibble, futility, and occasion for rebuke.
Yoga is the cessation of the fluctuations of the mind (Yogas chitta vritti nirodhah).
